new_document.description: Create a new untitled document. Adds a tab labeled "Untitled-N" to the canvas tab bar and a blank white canvas to the drawing surface. The new tab becomes active immediately. The tab's close button dispatches close_tab (which checks for unsaved changes). Tab index is captured at creation time via prop so click handlers remain correct as more tabs are added.

open_file.description: Show the native file-open dialog filtered to SVG files. If the chosen file is already open in a tab, switch to that tab instead of opening a duplicate. Otherwise create a new tab, parse the SVG, and display it on the canvas. The tab label shows the filename.

save.description: Save the active document to its current file path. If the document has never been saved (no filename), dispatches save_as to show a Save As dialog instead. After a successful save, clears the document's modified flag and removes the * indicator from the tab label.

save_as.description: Show the native file-save dialog for the active document. The user chooses a filename and location. On confirm, writes the document as SVG, updates the tab label to the new filename, and clears the modified flag.

revert.description: Reload the active document from its file on disk, discarding all unsaved changes since the last save. The canvas re-renders with the file contents, the undo stack is cleared, and the modified flag is reset. Only enabled when the document has a filename and has been modified.

quit.description: Close the application. If any open documents have unsaved changes, opens the save_changes_window dialog offering Save All, Save, Don't Save, or Cancel. If no documents are modified, closes immediately.

undo.description: Undo the last editing operation on the active document. Pops the most recent entry from the undo stack, reverses its effect on the canvas, and pushes it onto the redo stack. The canvas re-renders to reflect the prior state. Disabled when the undo stack is empty.

redo.description: Redo the last undone operation. Pops the most recent entry from the redo stack, re-applies its effect, and pushes it back onto the undo stack. Disabled when the redo stack is empty. Any new editing operation clears the redo stack.

cut.description: Copy the selected objects to the clipboard as SVG markup, then delete them from the canvas. Equivalent to copy followed by delete_selection. Disabled when nothing is selected.

copy.description: Copy the selected objects to the clipboard as SVG markup, preserving their positions, styles, and grouping. The original objects remain on the canvas. Disabled when nothing is selected.

paste.description: Paste objects from the clipboard into the active document, offset 24 points down and to the right from the original position. The pasted objects become the new selection. Repeated pastes stack with cumulative offsets. Disabled when no document is open.

paste_in_place.description: Paste objects from the clipboard at their original coordinates (no offset). Useful for pasting objects into the same position in a different document or layer. The pasted objects become the new selection.

select_all.description: Select all unlocked, visible objects in the active document. Hidden and locked objects are excluded from the selection.

delete_selection.description: Delete the selected objects from the canvas. The deletion is recorded on the undo stack. After deletion, the selection becomes empty.

group.description: Wrap the selected objects in a new Group element. The group inherits the z-order position of the frontmost selected object. The selection changes to the new group. Requires at least 2 selected objects.

ungroup.description: Ungroup the selected Group elements one level, replacing each group with its direct children at the group's z-order position. Non-group objects in the selection are left unchanged. The children become the new selection.

ungroup_all.description: Recursively ungroup every unlocked Group in the document until no groups remain (except locked ones). Operates on the entire document, not just the selection.

lock.description: Lock the selected elements to prevent editing. Locked objects cannot be selected, moved, resized, or deleted until unlocked. They remain visible on the canvas and in the Layers panel (shown with a lock icon). The selection is cleared after locking.

unlock_all.description: Unlock all locked elements in the active document, making them selectable and editable again. The lock icon is removed from their Layers panel entries.

hide_selection.description: Hide the selected elements from the canvas. Hidden objects are not rendered and cannot be selected, but remain in the document and Layers panel (shown dimmed with a hidden-eye icon). The selection is cleared after hiding.

show_all.description: Show all hidden elements in the active document, making them visible on the canvas and selectable again. Their eye icons are restored in the Layers panel.

zoom_in.description: Zoom the canvas in by one step (e.g. 150% -> 200%). Zoom is centered on the current viewport center. The zoom level is reflected in active_document.zoom_level.

zoom_out.description: Zoom the canvas out by one step (e.g. 200% -> 150%). Zoom is centered on the current viewport center.

fit_in_window.description: Adjust zoom and scroll so the bounding box of all objects in the document fits within the visible canvas area with a small margin. If the document is empty, resets to 100% zoom centered on the artboard origin.

select_tool.description: Switch the active drawing tool. Updates active_tool state, which causes the corresponding toolbar button to show its checked style and changes the canvas cursor to match the tool. The previously active tool is deactivated.

swap_fill_stroke.description: Swap the fill and stroke colors. The fill color becomes the stroke color and vice versa. Null values (no fill / no stroke) are swapped too. The toolbar swatches update immediately.

reset_fill_stroke.description: Reset colors and stroke attributes to defaults: white fill (#ffffff), black stroke (#000000), 1pt stroke width, butt cap, miter join, limit 10, center alignment, no dashing, no arrowheads, 100% scale, tip-at-end alignment, uniform profile, and fill swatch on top. The toolbar swatches update immediately.

toggle_fill_on_top.description: Toggle which swatch is in front in the toolbar fill/stroke widget. The front swatch is the one that receives color changes and is targeted by the None button. Visually the swatches swap z-order.

set_fill_none.description: Remove the fill color (set to null). Objects with no fill are transparent — only their stroke is visible. The fill swatch shows a red diagonal line indicating "none".

set_stroke_none.description: Remove the stroke color (set to null). Objects with no stroke have no visible outline. The stroke swatch shows a red diagonal line indicating "none".

open_color_picker.description: Open the modal color picker dialog for either the fill or stroke color (determined by the target param). The dialog initializes from the current color and commits back to the corresponding state variable when OK is pressed.

set_active_color.description: Set the active color (fill or stroke, determined by fill_on_top) to the given hex color value. Adds the color to the front of the per-document recent_colors list (max 10). If the color is already present, it moves to front rather than duplicating. This action is the commit point for all color changes from the Color Panel: swatch clicks (fixed and recent), slider release, hex field Enter/Tab, and color bar pointer-up.

set_active_color_none.description: Set the active attribute (fill or stroke, determined by fill_on_top) to none (null). Equivalent to set_fill_none when fill_on_top is true and set_stroke_none when false. The active attribute's swatch shows the none indicator (white square with red diagonal slash). Sliders and hex field in the Color Panel become disabled.

set_fill_type_solid.description: Sets the active attribute (fill or stroke, per fill_on_top) to solid color mode. If the active color is currently null, restores it to black (#000000). If it already has a solid color this is a no-op — the fill type is already solid.

set_fill_type_gradient.description: Sets the active attribute (fill or stroke, per fill_on_top) to gradient fill mode. Currently a placeholder — gradient editing is not yet implemented.

invert_active_color.description: Invert the active color (fill or stroke, per fill_on_top) by computing (255 − R, 255 − G, 255 − B) in RGB. The result is committed via set_active_color, which adds it to recent colors. The current color mode is unchanged. Disabled when the active attribute is none. Enabled only when the active color is non-null.
invert_active_color.enabled_when: if state.fill_on_top then state.fill_color != null else state.stroke_color != null

complement_active_color.description: Compute the complementary color of the active color (fill or stroke, per fill_on_top) by rotating the hue by 180° in HSB: result is ((H + 180) mod 360, S, B). The result is committed via set_active_color, which adds it to recent colors. The current color mode is unchanged. When saturation is 0 (achromatic gray), hue is undefined and the operation is a silent no-op — the color is unchanged. Disabled when the active attribute is none.
complement_active_color.enabled_when: if state.fill_on_top then state.fill_color != null else state.stroke_color != null

set_color_panel_mode.description: Set the Color Panel's color mode (panel-local state). The mode determines which slider group is displayed: Grayscale (K only), RGB (R/G/B 0–255), HSB (H 0–360, S/B 0–100), CMYK (C/M/Y/K 0–100), or Web Safe RGB (R/G/B 0–255 snapping to steps of 51). Changing the mode does not change the current color — only the view changes. The panel checkmark in the Color Panel menu moves to the new mode.

toggle_pane.description: Toggle a pane's visibility. When hidden, the pane is removed from the layout and adjacent panes may expand to fill the space. When shown, the pane reappears at its last position. Controlled via the Window menu checkmarks for Toolbar and Panels.

toggle_panel.description: Show or hide a dock panel via the Window menu. When showing, the panel is added to the last group of the anchored dock (dock_main), the dock is made visible and expanded, and the panel is activated. When hiding, the panel is removed from its group via close_panel. If the group becomes empty it is removed automatically.

toggle_canvas_maximize.description: Toggle the canvas pane between maximized (fills the entire viewport, title bar hidden) and its normal size within the pane layout.

maximize_canvas.description: Maximize the canvas pane to fill the entire viewport. The canvas title bar is hidden to maximize drawing space. Toolbar and dock panes float on top if visible. Double-click the canvas title bar or click the maximize button again to restore.

restore_canvas.description: Restore the canvas from maximized state to its normal pane size. The title bar reappears and all panes return to their default layout positions.

toggle_dock_collapse.description: Toggle the dock between expanded (showing full panel content) and collapsed (showing only the icon strip at collapsed_width).

collapse_dock.description: Collapse the dock to its narrow icon strip (collapsed_width). The dock_view switches to collapsed rendering: group headers and bodies are hidden, only the icon strip is shown. The chevron icon flips.

expand_dock.description: Expand the dock from the collapsed icon strip to full width. The dock_view switches to expanded rendering: group headers with tab bars, panel bodies, and separators are shown.

tile_panes.description: Tile all visible panes in a horizontal row filling the viewport. Panes with fixed_width keep their width. Panes with collapsed_width keep their current width. Flex panes split the remaining space equally (respecting min_width). All panes are set to full viewport height. Panes are sorted left-to-right by their current x position. Hidden panes are made visible.

save_workspace_as.description: Open the workspace_save_as dialog prompting the user for a layout name. The dialog validates the name and warns about overwrites.

save_workspace_confirm.description: Confirm the workspace save. Reads the name from the dialog text input, captures all pane positions/sizes and layout_state variables, and writes them to browser storage. The saved layout appears in the Workspace menu with a checkmark. Closes the dialog on success.

reset_workspace.description: Reset all pane positions, sizes, and layout_state variables to their declared defaults from the workspace YAML. Clears the active layout name (no layout is active after reset).

revert_workspace.description: Revert the pane layout to the currently active saved layout, discarding any manual repositioning or resizing done since the layout was loaded. Only enabled when a saved layout is active.

switch_workspace.description: Load a named workspace layout from storage, applying its saved pane positions/sizes and layout_state values. The layout name becomes the active layout (shown with a checkmark in the Workspace menu).

delete_workspace.description: Delete a user-saved workspace layout from storage. The layout is removed from the Workspace menu. Default layouts cannot be deleted. If the deleted layout was active, the active layout name is cleared.

switch_appearance.description: Switch to a named appearance, updating all CSS variables to the new colors, fonts, and sizes. The appearance name is shown with a checkmark in the Appearance menu.

save_appearance_as.description: Open the appearance_save_as dialog prompting the user for an appearance name. The current resolved appearance is saved to browser storage as a user-defined appearance.

save_appearance_confirm.description: Confirm the appearance save. Reads the name from the dialog, saves the current resolved appearance to browser storage, and closes the dialog.

close_tab.description: Close a document tab. If the document has unsaved changes, opens the save_changes_tab dialog offering Save, Discard, or Cancel. If the document is unmodified, dispatches remove_tab immediately to remove the tab and canvas elements.

remove_tab.description: Remove a tab and its canvas without prompting. Removes both the tab button and canvas surface elements, then selects an adjacent tab (prefer the tab to the left; if none, the tab to the right; if the last tab was closed, set active_tab to -1).

close_panel.description: Close (hide) a panel by removing it from its panel group. If the group becomes empty, it is removed from the dock. The panel can be shown again via the Window menu's panel toggle, which adds it back to the last group of the anchored dock.

show_panel.description: Show a hidden panel by adding it to the last group of the anchored dock. Makes the dock visible and expanded, and activates the newly shown panel.

dismiss_dialog.description: Dismiss the topmost modal dialog without taking any action. The dialog closes and focus returns to the main workspace.

close_without_saving.description: Close the tab or window without saving changes. Called from the save-changes dialog when the user clicks Discard or Don't Save. Closes the dialog first, then proceeds with the close operation, discarding any unsaved modifications.

save_and_close.description: Save the active document and then close its tab. Called from the save-changes dialog when the user clicks Save. Dispatches the save action first (which may show Save As if untitled), then closes the dialog and removes the tab.

save_all_and_close.description: Save all modified documents and then close the application window. Called from the save_changes_window dialog when the user clicks Save All. Iterates through all open tabs, saves any with unsaved changes, then closes the dialog and the application.

new_swatch.description: Create a new swatch in the active library using the current fill/stroke color (per fill_on_top). Opens the Swatch Options dialog in create mode, initialized from the active color.

params.color: if state.fill_on_top then state.fill_color else state.stroke_color

duplicate_swatch.description: Duplicate the currently selected swatch(es) in the active library. New swatches are inserted immediately after the originals with " copy" appended to the name.

delete_swatch.description: Delete the currently selected swatch(es) from the active library. Selection is cleared after deletion.

select_all_unused_swatches.description: Select all swatches in the panel whose color does not appear anywhere in the current document as a fill or stroke color.

add_used_colors.description: Scan the active document for all unique fill and stroke colors in use and add swatches for any that are not already present in the active swatch library. New swatches are appended to the end of the library.

sort_swatches_by_name.description: Permanently reorder swatches in the active library alphabetically by swatch name. This modifies the library data in place.

set_swatch_thumbnail_size.description: Set the thumbnail display size for the Swatches panel. Small = 16px, Medium = 32px, Large = 64px. Panel-local state.

open_swatch_options.description: Open the Swatch Options dialog. In edit mode, populates fields from the selected swatch (passed via params: swatch_name, color, color_mode, library, index). In create mode, populates from the current active color.

swatch_options_confirm.description: Confirm the Swatch Options dialog. In edit mode, updates the selected swatch with the dialog values. In create mode, appends a new swatch to the selected library. Closes the dialog.

save_swatch_library.description: Open the Save Swatch Library dialog prompting the user for a library name and saving the active library to workspace/swatches/.

save_swatch_library_confirm.description: Confirm saving the swatch library under the entered name. Closes the dialog on success.

set_stroke_cap.description: Set the line cap style for the active stroke. Butt: flat at the endpoint. Round: semicircular beyond the endpoint. Square: extends beyond the endpoint by half the stroke weight. Updates both panel state and global state (which propagates to the selection).

set_stroke_join.description: Set the line join style for the active stroke. Miter: sharp pointed corner (subject to miter limit). Round: circular arc. Bevel: flat diagonal cut. Updates both panel state and global state.

set_stroke_align.description: Set the stroke alignment relative to the path. Center: stroke straddles the path. Inside: stroke drawn inside closed paths. Outside: stroke drawn outside closed paths. Inside and outside behave as center on open paths. Updates both panel state and global state.

toggle_dashed_line.description: Toggle dashed stroke on or off. When disabled, the dash/gap fields are grayed out but retain their values. Updates both panel state and global state.

apply_dash_preset.description: Apply a dash pattern preset, enabling dashing and filling in the dash/gap fields. The even_dash preset sets 12pt dash, 12pt gap (pair 1 only). The dash_dot preset sets 12pt dash, 6pt gap, 0pt dash (dot), 6pt gap (pairs 1 and 2). Updates both panel state and global state.

swap_arrowheads.description: Swap the start and end arrowhead selections, including both the shape and the scale values. Updates both panel state and global state.

toggle_link_arrowhead_scale.description: Toggle linked arrowhead scales. When linked, changing either start or end arrowhead scale updates both to the same value. Updates both panel state and global state.

set_arrow_align.description: Set the arrow alignment mode. Tip-at-end: the arrowhead tip sits exactly at the path endpoint. Center-at-end: the arrowhead center sits at the endpoint, with the tip extending beyond. Updates both panel state and global state.

set_stroke_profile.description: Set the variable-width stroke profile. Uniform: constant width. Taper_both: tapers at both ends. Taper_start/taper_end: tapers at one end. Bulge: wider in the middle. Pinch: narrower in the middle. Updates both panel state and global state.

flip_stroke_profile.description: Flip (mirror) the stroke profile along the path. Only visually meaningful for asymmetric profiles (taper_start, taper_end). Updates both panel state and global state.

reset_stroke_profile.description: Reset the stroke profile to uniform and clear the flipped state. Updates both panel state and global state.

new_layer.description: Create a new layer and insert it immediately above the topmost panel-selected layer (or at the end if nothing is selected). The name is auto-generated as "Layer N", skipping numbers already in use. The new layer starts with default visibility (preview) and unlocked; one undo snapshot covers the operation.

new_group.description: Wrap the panel-selected elements in a new group at the position of the topmost panel-selected element. All panel-selected elements must share the same immediate parent container; the action is disabled otherwise. The new group is unnamed and inherits the parent container's properties. The panel selection changes to the new group.

delete_layer_selection.description: Delete the panel-selected elements from the document. Iterates in reverse path order to keep indices valid across deletions. Deleted elements are placed on the undo stack. After deletion, the panel selection becomes empty.

duplicate_layer_selection.description: Duplicate the panel-selected elements in place. Each duplicate is inserted immediately after its original in the same parent container. Iterates in reverse path order so each insert's indices stay valid for remaining iterations.

collect_in_new_layer.description: Move all panel-selected items into a new layer placed above all existing layers. The items are removed from their original locations and maintain their relative order. The new layer's name and color follow the same auto-generation rules as new_layer. Disabled during isolation mode.

flatten_artwork.description: Recursively unpack all groups in the panel-selected items until no groups remain. The children of each dissolved group take its position in the parent container. Enabled when the panel-selected items contain at least one group.

toggle_all_layers_visibility.description: Toggle all top-level layers between visible and invisible. If any layer is currently visible, all layers are set to invisible ("Hide All Layers"). If all layers are invisible, all layers are set to preview mode ("Show All Layers"). Takes one undo snapshot for the whole operation.

let.target:
if any(active_document.top_level_layers,
       fun l -> l.common.visibility != 'invisible')
  then 'invisible'
  else 'preview'

toggle_all_layers_outline.description: Toggle all top-level layers between preview and outline mode. If any layer is in preview mode, all layers are set to outline ("Outline All Layers"). If all layers are in outline mode, all layers are set to preview ("Preview All Layers"). Takes one undo snapshot for the whole operation.

let.target:
if any(active_document.top_level_layers,
       fun l -> l.common.visibility == 'preview')
  then 'outline'
  else 'preview'

toggle_all_layers_lock.description: Toggle all top-level layers between locked and unlocked. If any layer is unlocked, all layers are locked ("Lock All Layers"). If all layers are locked, all layers are unlocked ("Unlock All Layers"). Takes one undo snapshot for the whole operation.

cycle_element_visibility.description: Cycle the visibility of a single element through the three states: preview -> outline -> invisible -> preview. When applied to a container (layer or group), the visibility change affects all descendants.

solo_element_visibility.description: Solo/unsolo an element's visibility among its siblings (triggered by Option-click on the eye button). On first Option-click, the clicked element becomes the only visible sibling; all other siblings at the same level are set to invisible, and their previous visibility states are saved. On second Option-click of the same element, all siblings are restored to their saved visibility states. If the user manually changes any sibling's visibility between the two Option-clicks, the saved state is discarded.

toggle_element_lock.description: Toggle the lock state of a single element. When locking a container, the container's direct children's current lock states are saved, then all children are locked. When unlocking a container, each direct child's lock state is restored to its saved value. Locked elements cannot be selected or edited on the canvas, but can be dragged/reparented in the layers panel.

toggle_element_twirl.description: Toggle the twirl-down (expand/collapse) state of a container element (layer or group). The twirl state persists across sessions. Expanding reveals the container's children in the tree; collapsing hides them. Leaf elements do not have a twirl button.

enter_isolation_mode.description: Enter isolation mode for a container (layer or group). All content outside the isolated container is dimmed to 10% opacity and becomes non-interactive. The current visibility state of every element is saved before entering. Isolation is a stack: entering isolation while already in isolation pushes a new level. Panel selection and element selection are constrained to descendants of the isolated container. New Layer and Collect in New Layer are disabled.

let.target:
if param.container_id != null
  then path_from_id(param.container_id)
else if active_document.layers_panel_selection_count == 1
  then panel.layers_panel_selection[0]
else null

exit_isolation_mode.description: Exit one level of isolation mode. The visibility states saved on entry to the current level are restored. If at the outermost isolation level, isolation mode is fully exited.

exit_isolation_to_level.description: Exit isolation mode to a specific level, as triggered by clicking a breadcrumb segment. Pops all isolation levels above the target level, restoring saved visibility states for each popped level. A level_index of 0 exits isolation entirely.

layers_panel_select.description: Panel-select an element in the layers panel by clicking on its spacer, twirl-down gap, preview, or name area. Click selects the element and deselects all others. Shift-click extends the selection in visual (flattened) row order from the last-clicked element; selecting a collapsed group includes all its descendants. Command-click toggles the element in or out of the current selection. Panel selection is independent of element selection. In isolation mode, constrained to the isolated subtree.

layers_element_select.description: Element-select via clicking the SELECT_SQUARE in the layers panel. Click selects the element and deselects all others. Shift-click extends the selection in visual row order; selecting a collapsed group includes all its descendants. Command-click toggles the element. Elements can be selected across layers. The SELECT_SQUARE is filled with the color of the element's nearest ancestor layer when selected, otherwise empty.

layers_select_all.description: Select all visible elements in the layers panel (Command-A). In isolation mode, selects all visible elements within the isolated subtree. Applies to panel selection.

layers_nav_up.description: Move the panel-selection focus to the previous visible row in the layers panel. If at the first row, no-op.

layers_nav_down.description: Move the panel-selection focus to the next visible row in the layers panel. If at the last row, no-op.

layers_nav_right.description: If the focused row is a collapsed container, expand it. If it is an expanded container, move focus to its first child. If it is a leaf, no-op.

layers_nav_left.description: If the focused row is an expanded container, collapse it. If it is a collapsed container or a leaf, move focus to its parent. If already at a top-level layer, no-op.

rename_element.description: Begin inline renaming of the panel-selected element. Only available when exactly one element is panel-selected. The element name becomes an editable text field in place. Also triggered by double-clicking on the element name text.

confirm_rename.description: Confirm the inline rename (triggered by Enter or click-outside). If the name is empty, the element reverts to its auto-generated type name. Duplicate names are allowed. The rename is a single undoable action.

cancel_rename.description: Cancel the inline rename (triggered by Escape). The name reverts to its value before editing began.

set_layers_search.description: Update the search query string for the layers panel. The tree is incrementally filtered to show elements whose name (or auto-generated type name) contains the query string, case-insensitive. Ancestor nodes of matching elements are shown for context but dimmed. Collapsed nodes are searched and auto-expanded to reveal matches. Drag-and-drop is disabled while a search string is active.

toggle_layers_type_filter.description: Toggle an element type in the layers panel type filter. The filter dropdown has checkboxes for each element type (Layer, Group, Path, Rectangle, Circle, Ellipse, Polyline, Polygon, Text, Text Path, Line). Unchecking a type hides all elements of that type. Filtering composes with the search field. Ancestor nodes are still shown for context.

open_layer_options.description: Open the Layer Options dialog for an existing layer (edit mode) or for creating a new layer (create mode). In edit mode, the target layer's current name / visibility / locked state are resolved via element_at(path_from_id(layer_id)) and passed as dialog params so the dialog's init: block populates the inputs. In create mode, layer_id is null and the dialog's defaults take effect (name "Layer N", color preset, visible+preview+unlocked).

let.layer_elem:
if param.layer_id != null
  then element_at(path_from_id(param.layer_id))
  else null

params.name: if layer_elem != null then layer_elem.name else ''
params.lock: if layer_elem != null then layer_elem.common.locked else false

layer_options_confirm.description: Commit the Layer Options dialog. In create mode (param.layer_id is null) creates a new layer with the specified name, visibility, and lock state, inserted above the topmost panel-selected layer (or appended when nothing is selected). In edit mode (param.layer_id non-null) updates the existing layer at that path in place. Visibility is derived from the show and preview booleans: show=false maps to invisible; show=true with preview=true maps to preview; show=true with preview=false maps to outline. One undo snapshot covers the operation. Closes the dialog on success.

let.target_visibility: if not param.show then 'invisible' else if param.preview then 'preview' else 'outline'

scroll_to_selected.description: Auto-scroll the layers panel to reveal the element currently selected on the canvas. If the element is inside a collapsed group, the group and all ancestor groups are auto-expanded first. If multiple elements are selected, scrolls to the first selected element in document order.

toggle_hanging_punctuation.description: Toggle the Hanging Punctuation flag on the current paragraph selection. Phase 2 stub — real toggle of panel.hanging_punctuation and the jas:hanging-punctuation attribute on the wrapper tspan lands in Phase 4 alongside the panel-state mutation primitives.

open_paragraph_justification.description: Open the Justification dialog from the Paragraph panel menu. Dialog content lives in workspace/dialogs/paragraph_justification.yaml.

open_paragraph_hyphenation.description: Open the Hyphenation dialog from the Paragraph panel menu. Dialog content lives in workspace/dialogs/paragraph_hyphenation.yaml.

reset_paragraph_panel.description: Reset every Paragraph panel control to its default per PARAGRAPH.md §Reset Panel. Phase 2 stub — Phase 4 wires the real reset (panel-local state plus jas:* attrs on the selection's paragraph wrapper tspans).

app.name: Jas
app.description: Vector illustration application with draggable pane-based workspace, multi-document tabbed canvas, toolbar with tool slots and long-press alternates, and a collapsible dock with panel groups. Designed to be rendered by a generic web app using Bootstrap for layout and JavaScript for interaction.

layout.description: Three draggable, resizable panes with edge-snapping. Panes can be reordered in z-order by clicking. The canvas pane can be maximized to fill the viewport, causing toolbar and dock to float on top.

behavior.description: Begin dragging a pane by its title bar
Move pane to follow cursor. If any pane edge is within theme.sizes.snap_distance pixels of another pane's edge or the viewport boundary, snap to that edge and show a snap_preview colored guide line.
Drop the pane at its current position. Remove any snap preview guide lines.
Begin resizing a pane by its edge handle

Resize the pane by moving the dragged edge. Compute the delta from _resize_start to the current cursor position. For left/right edges, adjust the pane's left and/or width. For top/bottom edges, adjust top and/or height. Clamp to the pane's min_width/max_width and min_height constraints. Snap the edge to nearby pane edges within theme.sizes.snap_distance pixels.

children.description: Fixed-width (72px) vertical toolbar on the left side of the workspace. Contains a 2-column grid of 32x32px tool buttons followed by the fill/stroke color indicator widget. The pane can be dragged by its title bar, closed via the X button, and shown/hidden via the Window menu. Cannot be resized horizontally.
4-row, 2-column grid of 32x32px tool buttons. Single click selects the tool. Buttons with a small triangle indicator have alternate tools accessible via long-press (250ms hold).
Black arrow cursor for selecting, moving, and resizing objects. Click to select; Shift+click to add to selection; drag to move; drag handles to resize. No long-press alternates.

White arrow for selecting individual anchor points and path segments. Click an anchor to select it; drag to move it; drag control handles to adjust curves. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Partial Selection and Interior Selection tools. The button shows checked state when any tool in this slot is active.

items.label: Partial Selection
items.description: Select and move individual anchor points and path segments

items.label: Interior Selection
items.description: Select objects within groups without ungrouping

children.description: Draw precise paths by clicking to place anchor points. Click-and-drag to create smooth bezier curves with control handles. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Pen, Add Anchor Point, Delete Anchor Point, and Anchor Point tools.

items.label: Pen
items.description: Draw paths by clicking to place anchor points

items.label: Add Anchor Point
items.description: Add anchor points to an existing path

items.label: Delete Anchor Point
items.description: Remove anchor points from a path

items.label: Anchor Point
items.description: Convert anchor points between smooth and corner

children.description: Freehand drawing tool that creates paths by tracking mouse movement. The path is smoothed after drawing. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Pencil, Path Eraser, and Smooth tools.

Create and edit text objects. Click on the canvas to place a text insertion point; click-drag to create a text area box. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Type and Type on a Path tools.

items.label: Type on a Path
items.description: Place text along a path curve

children.description: Draw straight line segments. Click to place the start point, drag to the end point, and release. Hold Shift to constrain to 45-degree increments. No long-press alternates.

Draw rectangular shapes. Click-drag to define the bounding box. Hold Shift to constrain to a square; hold Alt/Option to draw from center. Long-press (250ms) opens a menu to switch between Rectangle, Rounded Rectangle, Polygon, and Star tools.
Freehand selection tool. Draw a closed lasso boundary around objects to select them. Any object whose bounds intersect the lasso path is included in the selection. No long-press alternates.

Central drawing area with a multi-tab document bar at the top. Each open document gets a tab labeled with its filename (or "Untitled-N" for new documents); a * prefix indicates unsaved changes. Clicking a tab switches the visible canvas. The drawing surface supports zoom (Ctrl+=/-, scroll wheel) and pan (scroll bars, Space+drag). The pane is a flex pane that expands to fill space between the toolbar and dock. Double-clicking the title bar maximizes the canvas to fill the viewport; double-click again or click the maximize button to restore.

Horizontal row of tab buttons at the top of the canvas pane. Starts empty; tabs are added dynamically by new_document and open_file actions via create_child. Each tab shows the document name and a close (X) button. Clicking a tab sets active_tab to that tab's index; the active tab gets highlighted styling. The tab bar is hidden when no documents are open (tab_count == 0).

Container for document canvases, filling the space below the tab bar. Each open document gets a canvas element here via create_child. Only the canvas matching active_tab is visible; the rest are hidden. Starts empty. The visible canvas is the interactive drawing surface where tools operate.

Brand logo shown in the top-right of the drawing surface when no documents are open. Spans 2 Bootstrap columns wide (16.67%) and is positioned in the top-right corner. Hidden once any document tab is open.

Right-side anchored dock pane containing a dock_view element. The dock_view renders panel groups with tab bars, collapse/expand chevrons, and hamburger menus automatically from its groups list. When collapsed, shows a narrow icon strip. Panel groups can be dragged out to create floating dock panes, or dragged between docks. The dock is resizable between 150px and 500px. Panel group state is saved with workspace layouts.

content.description: The primary anchored dock containing all panel groups. The dock_view element renders group headers (tab bars with panel tabs, collapse chevrons, hamburger menus), group bodies (active panel content from workspace/panels/*.yaml), group separators, and the collapsed icon strip. Panel tabs can be dragged to reorder within a group, move to another group's tab bar, or dropped on an empty area of a dock to create a new panel group at that position. Dragging a tab or group to empty space outside any dock creates a new floating dock pane. Groups can be dragged by their grip handle to reorder within the dock or detach as floating dock panes. Empty groups are removed automatically when their last panel is moved or closed. Empty floating docks are removed when their last group is emptied. Double-clicking a floating dock's title bar merges its groups back into this anchored dock.

items.label: Workspace
items.description: Lists saved workspace layouts with a checkmark on the active one. Includes Save As, Reset to Default, and Revert to Saved commands.

items.label: Appearance
items.description: Lists available UI appearances (color themes) with a checkmark on the active one. Predefined appearances are loaded from workspace/appearances/*.json. Users can save custom appearances via Save As.

appearance_save_as.description: Text input dialog for naming a custom appearance. The current resolved appearance (all colors, fonts, and sizes) is saved to browser storage under the given name. Save is disabled when the name field is empty.

color_picker.description: Modal color picker dialog matching the standard vector illustration application layout. Left column: saturation/brightness gradient + hue colorbar + web-safe checkbox. Center column: HSB fields with radio selectors + color preview swatch, RGB fields, CMYK fields, hex field. Right column: OK, Cancel, Color Swatches buttons. The radio buttons select which color channel the 2D gradient and colorbar visualize — e.g. selecting H maps hue to the colorbar and S/B to the gradient axes; selecting R maps red to the colorbar and G/B to the gradient axes.

radio_channel.description: Which color channel the gradient/colorbar visualize

layer_options.description:
Modal dialog for editing or creating a layer. Supports two modes, determined by the param.mode parameter:
Edit mode (param.mode == "edit"): Opened by double-clicking a layer row's options area or choosing "Options for Layer..." from the panel menu or context menu. All fields are populated from the layer's current properties. OK updates the layer in place.
Create mode (param.mode == "create"): Opened by choosing "New Layer..." from the panel menu. Name defaults to "Layer N" (skipping numbers already in use). Color defaults to the next color in the nine-color preset cycle. OK creates a new layer inserted above the topmost panel-selected layer, or at the top of the stack if nothing is panel-selected.
Layout (top to bottom): - Name: label (col-3) + text input (col-9) - Color: label (col-3) + dropdown of 9 preset colors plus "Custom"
  display-only label (col-6) + color swatch that opens Color Picker
  (col-3). When a preset is selected, the swatch updates to match.
  If the current color does not match any preset, the dropdown
  displays "Custom." If a custom color matches a preset, the
  dropdown switches to that preset name.
- Row: empty (col-3) + Template checkbox (col-3, disabled) + Lock
  checkbox (col-3)
- Row: empty (col-3) + Show checkbox (col-3) + Print checkbox
  (col-3, disabled)
- Row: empty (col-3) + Preview checkbox (col-3, disabled when Show
  unchecked) + Dim Images to checkbox with percentage input (col-3)
- Bottom: Cancel button + OK button (right-aligned)
Visibility mapping: The Show and Preview checkboxes map to the three-state visibility model: - Show unchecked -> invisible (Preview is disabled) - Show checked + Preview checked -> preview - Show checked + Preview unchecked -> outline

color_preset.description: Selected color preset, or 'custom' if no preset matches

preview.description: Preview mode (true) or outline mode (false). Disabled when show is false.

dim_images.description: Dim raster images in this layer

dim_percentage.description: Opacity percentage for dimmed raster images

behavior.description: When a preset is selected, update dialog.color to the preset's hex value. The "Custom" option is display-only and cannot be selected by the user; it appears automatically when the swatch color does not match any preset.

children.description: Click to open the Color Picker to set a custom layer color.

bind.disabled: true
true
not dialog.show
not dialog.dim_images

save_changes_tab.description: Confirmation dialog shown when closing a tab with unsaved changes. Three options: Save the document, Discard changes, or Cancel.

save_changes_window.description: Confirmation dialog shown when closing the window with one or more modified documents. Four options: Save All, Save (active only), Don't Save, Cancel.

swatch_library_save.description: Modal dialog for saving the selected swatch library to a file. The dialog presents a single text input for the library name. On confirm (Save button, dispatches save_swatch_library_confirm), the selected library's complete data (name, description, and all swatches with their name, color, color_mode, color_type, and global fields) is serialized to JSON and written to workspace/swatches/<name>.json. Save is disabled when the name field is empty.

bind.disabled: dialog.name == ""

swatch_options.description:
Modal dialog for editing or creating a named color swatch. Supports two modes, determined by the param.mode parameter:
Edit mode (param.mode == "edit"): Opened by double-clicking a library swatch tile or choosing Swatch Options... from the panel menu. All fields are populated from the selected swatch's data (name, color, color_mode) passed as dialog parameters. The color component sliders and hex field are initialized from param.color using the color conversion functions. OK updates the swatch in its library at the specified index.
Create mode (param.mode == "create"): Opened by double-clicking a non-library swatch (recent color, fill/stroke widget), choosing New Swatch from the panel menu, or choosing Create New Swatch... from the Color Panel menu. Fields are initialized from the active fill/stroke color. OK appends a new swatch to the selected library.
Layout (top to bottom): - Swatch Name: text input (col-3 label / col-9 input) - Color Type: dropdown, always "Process Color" (disabled, future use) - Global: checkbox (centered, disabled, deferred for future use) - Color Mode: dropdown (Grayscale/HSB/RGB/CMYK/Web Safe RGB). Changing
  the mode switches which slider group is visible via data-bind-visible
  on each slider container. The dialog change handler updates
  dialogState.color_mode and calls updateBindings().
- Color preview swatch: 40px square showing the current color via
  bind color="#dialog.hex"
- Sliders: one group per color mode, each containing rows of
  [label, slider, number_input]. Moving a slider updates
  dialogState via the dialog input handler, which recomputes the
  hex value from all components and calls updateBindings() to
  refresh the preview swatch and hex field.
- Hex field: 6-character hex string (RRGGBB, displayed with # prefix) - Bottom row: Preview checkbox (live document update, future),
  Cancel (dismiss_dialog), OK (swatch_options_confirm)

The dialog state includes working copies of all color components (h, s, b, r, g, bl, c, m, y, k, hex) and a _init_color field used during initialization. On open, the JS dialog init code detects _init_color and computes all component values from it using the color conversion functions (hsb_h, rgb_r, cmyk_c, etc.).

color.description: Canonical color value; all components derive from this

artboards_panel_content.description:
Artboards panel — initial placeholder implementation. A document has a set of artboards (print page regions) with unique numbers starting at 1 and editable names. New documents start with a single 612×792pt artboard.
This placeholder renders a single editable row (number + name) bound to panel-local state. Real support for multiple artboards, an artboards list, and the Artboard Dialog arrives alongside the Artboard Tool and document-level artboards implementation.

children.description: Artboard number (1-based).
User-editable artboard name.

character_panel_content.description:
Character formatting panel — initial placeholder implementation. Provides font family, style-name, font size, leading, kerning, tracking, vertical and horizontal scale, baseline shift, character rotation, six character toggle buttons (All Caps, Small Caps, Superscript, Subscript, Underline, Strikethrough), language, and anti-aliasing mode, all bound to panel-local state.
Everything here is a placeholder bound to panel-local state — no control is yet wired to per-tspan attributes on the selection. Real editing arrives alongside the tspan-aware Character panel work (set_character_attribute via the unified TSPAN.md algorithm, mixed-value display for character-range selections, parens-default display for computed defaults, and the Snap to Glyph section) once that plumbing is in place across all four native apps.

color_panel_content.description:
Inline color panel for editing the active fill or stroke color (whichever swatch is in front, per fill_on_top). The panel has five color modes (Grayscale, RGB, HSB, CMYK, Web Safe RGB) stored as panel-local state. Changing the mode shows the appropriate slider set without changing the current color. The mode is re-initialized from the active color when the panel first opens.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 — Swatches. Three fixed shortcuts (None, Black, White) followed by a thin vertical rule and ten recent-color slots. Recent colors are per-document and shared between fill and stroke. Colors are added to the front of the list on commit (slider release, hex Enter, or swatch click). Duplicates move to front rather than duplicating. Empty slots render as hollow squares with a solid border.
Row 2 — Controls (two columns). Left column: the standard fill/stroke widget (overlapping swatches + swap + reset buttons), same visual behavior as the toolbar widget except single-click only (no double-click to open the modal picker). Right column: sliders for the active color mode.
Row 3 — Hex field. A six-character hex input (RRGGBB, displayed with a leading # label). Committing updates the active color and adds it to recent colors. In Web Safe RGB mode, the value snaps to the nearest web-safe color.
Bottom: a 64 px tall color gradient (color bar). Hue varies along the x-axis (0–360°). The y-axis is split into two halves: in the top half, saturation increases from 0% to 100% while brightness decreases from 100% to 80%; in the bottom half, saturation stays at 100% while brightness decreases from 80% to 0%. This produces a gradient that transitions from white/pastel at the top through fully saturated colors in the middle to black at the bottom. Clicking or dragging sets the active color, committing to the recent-colors list on pointer-up.
Sliders and the hex field are disabled (non-interactive) when the active attribute is none. Clicking any swatch (fixed or recent) implicitly un-nones the attribute and commits the swatch color.
Panel menu: five color-mode entries (with a checkmark on the active mode), Invert (255−R, 255−G, 255−B), Complement ((H+180) mod 360, S, B — no-op if S=0), and a permanently disabled Create New Swatch… entry.

hex.description: Working hex string (6 chars, no # prefix)

recent_colors.description: Per-document list of recently committed colors, newest first. Max 10 entries. A duplicate color moves to front rather than duplicating. Added on commit: slider release, hex field Enter, swatch click (including none/black/white). Not added for Invert or Complement — those dispatch set_active_color which adds there. Empty slots render as hollow squares with a solid border.

layers_panel_content.description:
Layer hierarchy panel displaying all elements in the document as an interactive tree. Each element occupies one row. The panel supports two independent selection models (element selection and panel selection), drag-and-drop reordering, inline renaming, search and filter, isolation mode, and a Layer Options dialog.
=== Row Layout ===
Each row is laid out left to right as:
SPACER — EYE_BUTTON — LOCK_BUTTON — TWIRL_DOWN_BUTTON — ELEMENT_PREVIEW — ELEMENT_NAME — SELECT_SQUARE
SPACER provides indentation in increments of 16px. Top-level layers are not indented. Each nesting level adds 16px: direct children of a layer are indented 16px, grandchildren 32px, and so on.
EYE_BUTTON displays and controls the element's visibility state (see Visibility below).
LOCK_BUTTON displays and controls whether the element is locked (see Locking below).
TWIRL_DOWN_BUTTON is present on layers and groups; clicking it expands or collapses the container's children in the tree (see Twirl-Down below). Leaf elements (paths, text, images, etc.) do not have a twirl button; a blank gap of the same width preserves column alignment.
ELEMENT_PREVIEW is a 32px square rasterized thumbnail of the element scaled to fit, rendered on a white background. Thumbnails are refreshed as elements change on a best-effort basis. Elements with outline or invisible visibility modes are rendered as if in preview mode for the thumbnail. Empty groups and layers show a blank preview.
ELEMENT_NAME is the name of the element, if it has one. Unnamed elements display their element type in angle brackets, truncated with ellipsis if the name exceeds the available space. The display names are: <Line>, <Rectangle>, <Circle>, <Ellipse>, <Polyline>, <Polygon>, <Path>, <Text>, <Text Path>, <Group>, <Layer>.
SELECT_SQUARE is a 12px square. It is filled with the color of the element's nearest ancestor layer if the element is currently element-selected (see Element Selection below). Otherwise it is empty (transparent fill with a thin border).
=== Visibility ===
Each element has one of three visibility states: preview (normal rendering), outline (wireframe rendering), or invisible (not rendered).
Clicking the EYE_BUTTON cycles visibility: preview -> outline -> invisible -> preview. The icon changes to match: eye_preview (open eye), eye_outline (eye with dashed pupil), or eye_invisible (closed/struck-through eye).
When a container (layer or group) changes visibility, the change applies to all its descendants.
Option-click (Alt-click) on the EYE_BUTTON triggers solo/unsolo among the clicked element's siblings at the same tree level. On the first Option-click, the clicked element becomes the only visible sibling; all other siblings are set to invisible and their previous visibility states are saved. On a second Option-click of the same element, all siblings are restored to their saved visibility states. If the user manually changes any sibling's visibility between the two Option-clicks, the saved state is discarded (no restore).
=== Locking ===
Clicking the LOCK_BUTTON toggles the element's lock state. Locked elements cannot be selected or edited on the canvas but remain visible. The icon shows lock_locked when locked, lock_unlocked when unlocked.
When locking a container, the container saves each direct child's current lock state, then sets all children to locked. When unlocking a container, each direct child's lock state is restored to the value saved at the time the container was locked. This is per-container: each container remembers only its own direct children's states.
Locked elements can still be dragged and reparented in the layers panel (lock protects content editing, not organizational position).
=== Twirl-Down ===
Layers and groups have a TWIRL_DOWN_BUTTON, expanded (open) by default. The icon shows twirl_open when expanded and twirl_closed when collapsed. Twirl state persists across sessions. Leaf elements do not have a twirl button; a blank gap preserves alignment. Empty layers and groups still show an active twirl button.
=== Element Selection ===
Element selection is the document-level selection, synchronized with the canvas. Clicking on the SELECT_SQUARE selects that element and deselects all others. Shift-click extends the selection in visual (flattened) row order from the last-clicked element to the clicked element. Selecting a collapsed group includes all its descendants. Command-click toggles the clicked element in or out of the current selection. Elements can be selected across layers.
When an element is selected on the canvas, the panel auto-scrolls to ensure that element's row is visible. If the element is inside a collapsed group, the group and all ancestor groups are auto-expanded first. If multiple elements are selected, the panel scrolls to the first selected element in document order.
=== Panel Selection ===
Panel selection is independent from element selection and is local to the layers panel. An element is panel-selected by clicking on its spacer, twirl-down gap, preview, or name area. Panel-selected elements are highlighted with the system selection color as a background on the row. Click selects one element and deselects all others. Shift-click extends the selection in visual row order; selecting a collapsed group includes all its descendants. Command-click toggles the element. In isolation mode, panel selection is constrained to the isolated subtree.
Panel-selected elements can be moved within the panel by dragging (see Drag-and-Drop below). All menu operations that refer to "selected" items act on panel selection, not element selection.
Right-clicking a row opens the context menu. If the right-clicked element is not already panel-selected, it becomes the sole panel-selected element first, then the menu acts on the selection.
=== Drag-and-Drop ===
Panel-selected elements can be dragged to reorder or reparent elements in the document. A horizontal line between rows indicates the drop insertion point.
Constraints: - Layers can be dragged to the top level or into other layers.
  Layers cannot be dragged into groups.
- Groups can be dragged into layers and other groups. - Leaf elements can be dragged into layers and groups. - Nothing can be dragged into a locked container. - Nothing can be dragged into itself or its own descendants. - Every element must live inside a layer; elements cannot be dragged
  to the root level unless they are layers.
- When dragging multiple panel-selected elements, all constraints
  must be satisfied for every element. If any element violates a
  constraint, the entire drop is rejected.
- Locked elements can be dragged (lock protects content, not
  position).

Hovering over a collapsed container for 500ms auto-expands it. Elements preserve their relative order when moved. Drag-and-drop is disabled while a search query is active.
=== Search and Filter ===
A search field appears at the top of the panel. Typing in the field incrementally filters the tree to show elements whose name (or auto-generated type name like <Path>) contains the search string, case-insensitive. Ancestor nodes of matching elements are shown to preserve tree context but are dimmed to distinguish them from actual matches. Clearing the field restores the full tree.
A filter button to the right of the search field opens a dropdown with checkboxes for each element type: Layer, Group, Path, Rectangle, Circle, Ellipse, Polyline, Polygon, Text, Text Path, Line. By default all types are checked. Unchecking a type hides all elements of that type. Filtering composes with the search field — both must match for an element to appear.
Collapsed nodes are searched; if a match is inside a collapsed group, the group is auto-expanded to reveal it. Panel selection and element selection are constrained to visible (matching) rows only. In isolation mode, search and filter operate within the isolated subtree only.
=== Isolation Mode ===
Isolation mode restricts editing to a single container and its descendants. Enter isolation by double-clicking a layer or group row (outside the name text area), or via the menu/context menu "Enter Isolation Mode" (enabled when exactly one container is panel-selected).
All content outside the isolated container is dimmed to 10% opacity and becomes non-interactive (cannot be selected, edited, or targeted). The dimmed content remains visible as spatial context.
Isolation is a stack. While in isolation, double-clicking a nested group enters a second level. The outer container also dims, leaving only the inner group's children fully interactive.
A breadcrumb bar appears at the top of the panel showing the isolation path (e.g., "Layer 1 > Group > Inner Group"). Each breadcrumb segment is clickable — clicking a segment exits isolation to that level. The root segment exits isolation entirely.
Exiting isolation: - Escape pops one level. At the outermost level, exits entirely. - Double-click an empty area of the canvas exits one level. - Menu/context menu "Exit Isolation Mode" exits one level. - Click a breadcrumb segment exits to that level directly.
On entering each isolation level, the visibility state of every element is saved. On exit, each element's visibility is restored. If the user changes a visibility state while in isolation, that change is preserved on exit (overwrites the saved value for that element).
Constraints during isolation: - Element selection constrained to the isolated container's
  descendants.
- Panel selection constrained to the isolated subtree. - Drag-and-drop constrained to within the isolated subtree. - "New Layer" and "Collect in New Layer" are disabled. - "New Group" operates within the isolated container.
=== Keyboard Shortcuts ===
- Delete / Backspace: delete panel-selected elements. - F2 or Enter: start renaming the panel-selected element (single
  selection only).
- Command-A: select all visible elements in the panel. - Up arrow: move focus to the previous visible row. - Down arrow: move focus to the next visible row. - Right arrow: expand a collapsed container, or move to its first
  child if already expanded.
- Left arrow: collapse an expanded container, or move to the parent
  if already collapsed or a leaf.
- Escape: exit one level of isolation mode, or cancel an active
  rename.

=== Renaming ===
Renaming is triggered by F2, Enter (when exactly one element is panel-selected), or double-clicking the element name text. The name becomes an editable text field in place. Confirm by pressing Enter or clicking outside the field. Cancel by pressing Escape (reverts to the value before editing). Confirming with an empty string removes the custom name — the element reverts to its auto-generated type name. Duplicate names are allowed. Renaming is a single undoable action.
Double-click on the name text starts rename; double-click on the row area outside the name enters isolation mode (for containers).
=== Empty State ===
A document always has at least one layer. Attempting to delete the last remaining layer is prevented (delete action is disabled). New documents start with a single layer named "Layer 1" with the first color in the preset cycle (Light Blue). No special empty-state message or placeholder is shown.
=== Layer Color Presets ===
The nine preset colors, in cycle order for auto-assignment: 1. Light Blue   (#4a90d9) 2. Red          (#d94a4a) 3. Green        (#4ad94a) 4. Blue         (#4a4ad9) 5. Yellow       (#d9d94a) 6. Magenta      (#d94ad9) 7. Cyan         (#4ad9d9) 8. Light Gray   (#b0b0b0) 9. Dark Green   (#2a7a2a)
Auto-assignment cycles through this list in order: Layer 1 gets Light Blue, Layer 2 gets Red, etc., wrapping after 9. The cycle index is per-document and advances with each layer created.
When a layer's color matches a preset, the Layer Options dialog dropdown shows that preset name. Otherwise it displays "Custom." "Custom" is a display-only label, not selectable from the dropdown; the user must click the color swatch to pick a custom color.

paragraph_panel_content.description:
Paragraph formatting panel per PARAGRAPH.md (Phase 2 — static UI). Provides the alignment radio group (7 icon toggles), bullets and numbered-list dropdowns, indent and space-before/after combos, and the hyphenate checkbox. Panel menu opens the Justification and Hyphenation dialogs; Hanging Punctuation is a menu-only checkmark toggle. Bound to panel-local state only; selection-driven wiring lands in Phase 3, paragraph attribute writes in Phase 4.
Layout follows PARAGRAPH.md §Layout — bootstrap-style grid with intentional right-padding on three rows (alignment: 5-col padding; first-line indent: 6-col padding; hyphenate: 9-col padding).

justify_left.description: JUSTIFY_LEFT_BUTTON — justify body, last line left.

justify_center.description: JUSTIFY_CENTER_BUTTON — justify body, last line centred.

justify_right.description: JUSTIFY_RIGHT_BUTTON — justify body, last line right.

justify_all.description: JUSTIFY_ALL_BUTTON — every line justified, including the last.

numbered_list.description: Numbered-list style. One of: num-decimal, num-lower-alpha, num-upper-alpha, num-lower-roman, num-upper-roman; empty = None.

first_line_indent.description: FIRST_LINE_INDENT_DROPDOWN. **Signed**, pt; range −1296 to +1296. Negative values produce hanging indents.

hyphenate.description: HYPHENATE_CHECKBOX (master). Mirrored by HYPHENATE_MASTER_CHECKBOX inside the Hyphenation dialog — both surfaces write panel.hyphenate.

properties_panel_content.description: Transform and appearance properties for the selected object(s): position (X, Y), size (W, H) with constrain-proportions lock, rotation angle, shear, opacity slider, and blend mode dropdown. When multiple objects are selected, shows shared values or blank for mixed values. All fields are editable and apply immediately.

stroke_panel_content.description:
Stroke attribute controls for the selected path(s). The panel provides controls for stroke weight, endpoint cap style, corner join style with miter limit, stroke alignment relative to the path, dashed line patterns, arrowheads with independent start/end shape and scale, arrow alignment mode, and variable-width stroke profiles.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 — Weight. A label and a number input for the stroke weight in points. Accepts any non-negative value including decimals.
Row 2 — Cap. Three mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons for line endpoint style: butt (flat at the endpoint), round (semicircular beyond the endpoint), and square (extends beyond the endpoint by half the stroke width). Default is butt.
Row 3 — Corner. Three mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons for line join style: miter (sharp corner), round (circular arc), and bevel (flat diagonal). Followed by a miter limit label and numeric input. The miter limit controls when a miter join auto-converts to bevel on acute angles; the value is the ratio of miter length to stroke width. Default miter limit is 10. The miter limit field is disabled when the join style is round or bevel.
Row 4 — Align Stroke. Three mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons controlling where the stroke is drawn relative to the path: center (stroke straddles the path, the default), inside (stroke drawn inside closed paths), or outside (stroke drawn outside closed paths). Inside and outside behave the same as center on open paths.
Row 5 — Dashed Line. A checkbox to enable dashing, plus two preset buttons: even dash (12pt/12pt) and dash-dot (12pt/6pt/0pt/6pt). Clicking a preset enables dashing and fills in the dash/gap fields.
Row 6 — Dash/gap pattern. Six numeric inputs arranged as three dash/gap pairs. Pair 1 defaults to 12pt/12pt. Pairs 2 and 3 default to blank (unused — not part of the pattern). A value of 0 in a dash position produces a zero-length segment (a dot with round caps). The effective dash array is built left to right from non-blank pairs. All six fields are disabled (grayed out) when the dashed line checkbox is unchecked. The values persist and reappear when re-checked.
Row 7 — Arrowheads. A label, two dropdowns (start and end) selecting from 15 arrowhead shapes, and a swap button that exchanges start and end arrowhead selections (both shape and scale). Both dropdowns share the same shape list. The renderer flips the shape based on which end it is placed on.
Arrowhead shapes: none (default), simple_arrow (filled triangle), open_arrow (unfilled triangle), closed_arrow (filled triangle with bar at base), stealth_arrow (sharp swept-back chevron, filled), barbed_arrow (curved swept-back, filled), half_arrow_upper (upper half of filled triangle), half_arrow_lower (lower half of filled triangle), circle (filled), open_circle (outline), square (filled), open_square (outline), diamond (filled diamond/rhombus), open_diamond (outline diamond), slash (perpendicular line across the path).
Row 8 — Scale. A label, two editable combo boxes (start and end) for arrowhead scale as a percentage, and a link/unlink toggle button (chain icon). Preset dropdown values: 50%, 75%, 100%, 150%, 200%, 300%, 400%. The user can also type any positive value (minimum 1%). Default is 100%. Scale is relative to the stroke weight. When the link button is active, changing either scale updates both. Default is unlinked.
Row 9 — Arrow Align. A label and two mutually exclusive icon toggle buttons: tip-at-end (the arrowhead tip sits exactly at the path endpoint; the body extends inward along the path) and center-at-end (the arrowhead center sits at the path endpoint; the tip extends beyond). Default is tip-at-end.
Row 10 — Profile. A dropdown selecting a variable-width stroke profile, plus flip and reset buttons. Profiles: uniform (constant width, the default), taper_both (tapers at both ends), taper_start (tapers at start), taper_end (tapers at end), bulge (wider in the middle), pinch (narrower in the middle). Flip mirrors the profile along the path. Reset restores uniform.

miter_limit.description: Miter length / stroke width ratio. When exceeded, the miter join auto-converts to bevel. Minimum 1. Only effective when join is miter.

align_stroke.description: Stroke alignment relative to the path. Center: stroke straddles the path. Inside: stroke drawn inside closed paths. Outside: stroke drawn outside closed paths. Inside and outside behave as center on open paths.

arrow_align.description: Arrow alignment mode. Tip-at-end: the arrowhead tip sits exactly at the path endpoint, body extends inward. Center-at-end: the arrowhead center sits at the endpoint, tip extends beyond.

swatches_panel_content.description:
Swatch library panel for managing named color swatches organized into libraries. The panel displays a fill/stroke widget, a recent-colors row, and one or more swatch libraries rendered as collapsible sections with wrapping color tiles.
Layout (top to bottom):
Row 1 -- Fill/stroke widget. Same visual behavior as the toolbar widget except single-click only (no double-click to open the modal picker from within the panel).
Row 2 -- "Recent Colors" label.
Row 3 -- Recent colors. Same per-document recent colors as the Color panel: ten 16px slots, newest first. Click sets the active color. Double-click opens the Swatch Options dialog in create mode.
Row 4 -- Swatch tiles area. Each loaded library is rendered as a collapsible section: a disclosure triangle + library name label, followed by swatches wrapping left-to-right, top-to-bottom. The tile size is controlled by the thumbnail_size panel state (small=16px, medium=32px, large=64px).
Single-click a library swatch to select it and set the active color. Double-click opens the Swatch Options dialog in edit mode. Shift+click adds a range to the selection. Cmd+click toggles individual swatch selection. Multiple swatches can be selected.
Each swatch has: name (string), color (hex), color_mode (grayscale/rgb/ hsb/cmyk/web_safe_rgb), color_type ("process"), global (bool, deferred).
Libraries are loaded from workspace/swatches/*.json at startup. The default library is "Web Colors" (216 web-safe colors).

thumbnail_size.description: Swatch tile display size. Small=16px, Medium=32px, Large=64px.

selected_library.description: ID of the library containing the most recent swatch selection. Used as the target for New Swatch, Sort, Save, etc.

selected_swatches.description: List of selected swatch indices within the selected library.

open_libraries.description: List of open libraries with their collapsed state. Each entry has an "id" (library filename stem) and "collapsed" (bool).

recent_colors.description: Per-document list of recently committed colors, newest first. Shared with the Color panel.

menu.label: New Swatch
menu.description: Appends a new swatch to the selected library, initialized from the current active color (fill or stroke, per fill_on_top). Opens the Swatch Options dialog in create mode so the user can edit the name, color mode, and color before confirming. The new swatch is appended at the end of the library's swatch list.

menu.label: Duplicate Swatch
menu.description: Create a copy of each currently selected swatch in the selected library. The copies are inserted immediately after their originals with " copy" appended to the name. The copies become the new selection. Disabled when no swatches are selected.

menu.label: Delete Swatch
menu.description: Remove the currently selected swatch(es) from the selected library. The deletion is permanent (no undo). The selection is cleared after deletion. Disabled when no swatches are selected.

menu.label: Select All Unused
menu.description: Select every swatch in the panel whose color does not appear anywhere in the current document as a fill or stroke color on any object. Useful for identifying swatches that can be safely deleted. The selection replaces any existing selection.

menu.label: Add Used Colors
menu.description: Scan every object in the current document and collect the set of unique fill and stroke colors in use. For each color that is not already present in the selected library, create a new swatch and append it to the end. Swatch names default to the "R=N G=N B=N" component format. Colors are compared by hex value.

menu.label: Sort by Name
menu.description: Permanently reorder the swatches in the selected library alphabetically by swatch name (case-sensitive, lexicographic). This modifies the library data in place. The reordered sequence is preserved when the library is saved.

menu.label: Small Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display swatch tiles at 16x16 pixels. This is the smallest view, matching the size of recent-color swatches. Radio button group with Medium and Large; a checkmark indicates the active size.

menu.label: Medium Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display swatch tiles at 32x32 pixels. Radio button group with Small and Large; a checkmark indicates the active size.

menu.label: Large Thumbnail View
menu.description: Display swatch tiles at 64x64 pixels. This is the largest view, showing colors with enough area to distinguish subtle variations. Radio button group with Small and Medium; a checkmark indicates the active size.

menu.label: Swatch Options...
menu.description: Open the Swatch Options dialog in edit mode for the first selected swatch. The dialog displays the swatch's name, color type (always "Process Color"), color mode, color component sliders, and hex value. OK updates the swatch in place. Disabled when no swatches are selected.

menu.label: Open Swatch Library
menu.description: Submenu listing all available swatch libraries discovered from workspace/swatches/*.json files. Selecting a library adds it to the panel's open_libraries list, displaying it as a new collapsible section in the swatch tiles area. Libraries that are already open are shown with a checkmark. Each library appears as a disclosure section with a triangle toggle and the library name.

menu.label: Save Swatch Library
menu.description: Open the Save Swatch Library dialog, which prompts for a library name. The selected library's swatches are saved to workspace/swatches/ as a JSON file. The file contains the library name, description, and the full swatch array (name, color, color mode, color type, global flag for each swatch).

children.description:
Scrollable area displaying all open swatch libraries. Each library is rendered as a collapsible disclosure section: a disclosure triangle (click to expand/collapse) followed by the library name label. When expanded, the library's swatches are displayed in a wrapping left-to-right, top-to-bottom layout (like text in a paragraph).
Multiple libraries can be open simultaneously, stacked vertically. The list of open libraries and their collapsed state is stored in panel.open_libraries. New libraries are added via the Open Swatch Library submenu in the panel menu.
Swatch tile size is controlled by panel.thumbnail_size (small=16px, medium=32px, large=64px), defaulting to small (16px) to match the recent-color swatches.
Interaction model: Single-click a swatch to select it and set the active fill/stroke color (per fill_on_top). Selection uses the generic select effect with mode=auto: plain click for single selection, Shift+click to extend a range, Cmd/Ctrl+click to toggle. The selected_swatches panel state tracks the selection as a list of indices within the selected_library. Visual selection feedback is provided by the data-bind-selected-in binding, which adds a jas-selected CSS class (2px accent-colored outline).
Double-click a swatch to open the Swatch Options dialog in edit mode, passing the swatch's name, color, color_mode, library id, and index as dialog parameters.
This area uses the generic repeat directive to iterate over panel.open_libraries (outer repeat, producing disclosure sections) and data.swatch_libraries[lib.id].swatches (inner repeat, producing color_swatch tiles). The bracket notation [lib.id] resolves the outer repeat variable within the inner repeat's data source path.

radio_field_row.description: Radio button + label + number input row for color picker HSB/RGB fields

cmyk_field_row.description: Label + number input + unit row for color picker CMYK fields

fill_stroke_widget.description: Overlapping fill/stroke color swatches with swap and reset buttons, plus Color/Gradient/None mode buttons at the bottom. All positions and sizes are derived from a single 'size' parameter.

slider_row.description: Labeled slider + number input row for color dialogs and panels
